rationale: In the citing paper, Agrawal and Duhachek (2010) appears in an introductory grouped citation as one example of prior work on health message framing effects. The paper does not discuss that study's specific shame-guilt compatibility findings, mechanism, measures, or design when developing hypotheses or interpreting results. Core theory and process tests in the citing paper are built using other citations tied directly to moral identity, stigma, motivated reasoning, and self-affirmation. So this reference functions as broad background rather than a distinct building block.
